title: Array covariance should be avoided
description: Array covariance should not be usedAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearmaintainabilityCode SmellpitfallWhy is this an issue?Array covariance is the principle that if an implicit or explicit reference conversion exits from typeAtoB, then the same conversion exists from the array typeA[]toB[].While this array conversion can be useful in readonly situations to pass instances ofA[]whereB[]is expected, it must be used with care, since assigning an instance ofBinto an array ofAwill cause anArrayTypeMismatchExceptionto be thrown at runtime.Noncompliant code exampleabstract class Fruit { } class Apple : Fruit { } class Orange : Fruit { } class Program { static void Main(string[] args) { Fruit[] fruits = new Apple[1]; // Noncompliant - array covariance is used FillWithOranges(fruits); } // Just lo
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.
